name: Canning Snap & Italian Bean Pieces (Green & W
recipeInstructions:
1. For a canner load of 7 quarts, approximately 14 pounds of beans are needed.
2. For a canner load of 9 pints, about 9 pounds of beans are required.
3. A bushel of beans weighs 30 pounds and yields 12 to 20 quarts, averaging 2 pounds per quart.
4. Select filled but tender, crisp green pods, discarding any diseased or rusty ones.
5. Wash the beans and trim the ends. Leave them whole or cut into 1-inch pieces.
6. For hot packing:
a. Cover the beans with boiling water.
b. Boil for 5 minutes.
c. Fill jars loosely, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
7. For raw packing:
a. Fill jars tightly with raw beans, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
b. Add 1 teaspoon of canning salt per quart, if desired.
c. Add boiling water, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
8. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2 based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for Snap and Italian Beans (dial-gauge pressure canner)
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 20 minutes for Pints, 25 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
- 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
- 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb
- 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb
- 6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb
Table 2: Recommended processing time for Snap and Italian Beans (weighted-gauge pressure canner)
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 20 minutes for Pints, 25 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
- 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
